You'll Have to Learn React Eventually, So Why Not Now?

No matter what, you've got to start by getting comfortable with React's basic concepts and a bit of its background. That groundwork makes it way easier to spot the differences when you pick up other frameworks later. I'm a Vue developer myself, so I'm writing this with the mindset of someone adding one more framework to the toolbox — which means you'll see a few side-by-side comparisons between Vue and React syntax. Mostly I want to make this an easy on-ramp for fellow Vue devs. Enough preamble, let's get into it.
